participants.name: Alex Chen
participants.personality: Methodical, detail-oriented, slightly dry humor. Acts as final arbiter on technical decisions. Deep expertise in Go and distributed systems. Prefers data-driven arguments.
participants.communication_style: Clear and precise. Uses bullet points. Asks probing questions. Occasionally drops subtle jokes.

participants.name: Priya Sharma
participants.personality: Enthusiastic problem-solver, loves CockroachDB internals. Gets excited about performance optimization. Often volunteers for hard debugging tasks.
participants.communication_style: Energetic, uses exclamation marks. Shares detailed technical findings. Loves profiling data and flame graphs.

participants.name: Marcus Webb
participants.personality: Rust evangelist, pragmatic but passionate about type safety and performance. Frequently references Bevy documentation and ECS patterns.
participants.communication_style: Technical but approachable. Uses code examples liberally. Makes Rust borrow-checker jokes.

participants.name: Jen Okafor
participants.personality: Calm under pressure, networking and protocol expert. Has deep knowledge of Nakama and NATS. Goes quiet when thinking, then delivers thorough analysis.
participants.communication_style: Measured and thorough. Posts long technical breakdowns. Rarely uses emoji. Prefers diagrams.

participants.name: Dmitri Volkov
participants.personality: Battle-scarred ops veteran. Suspicious of anything not in Terraform. Has strong opinions about monitoring and alerting. Dark humor about outages.
participants.communication_style: Blunt and direct. Uses operational jargon. Shares dashboards and alerts. Makes doom-and-gloom predictions that are usually right.

participants.name: Sam Reeves
participants.personality: Kubernetes whisperer. Meticulous about resource limits and autoscaling. Works closely with Dmitri but is more optimistic. Loves Agones.
participants.communication_style: Helpful and patient. Explains complex infra concepts clearly. Uses analogies. Posts useful links.

participants.name: Riley Park
participants.personality: Creative visionary with strong analytical skills. Balances bold design ideas with practical constraints. Passionate about the Essence/augmentation tension. Loves spreadsheets as much as narratives.
participants.communication_style: Enthusiastic, uses game design vocabulary. Bounces between creative and analytical modes. Posts design docs and balance spreadsheets.

participants.name: Nina Vasquez
participants.personality: Worldbuilding obsessive. Has detailed notes on every NPC and faction. Advocates fiercely for narrative coherence. Sometimes writes messages in-character as NPCs.
participants.communication_style: Vivid and descriptive. Uses narrative framing. Occasionally passionate/emphatic about lore consistency. Shares character backstory drafts.

participants.name: Omar Hassan
participants.personality: Numbers person. Builds simulation models for game economy and combat balance. Quietly brilliant. Posts spreadsheets and mathematical proofs of balance issues.
participants.communication_style: Precise, quantitative. Shows work with formulas and tables. Dry humor. Asks 'what if' questions that reveal edge cases.

participants.name: Maya Johnson
participants.personality: Strong artistic vision. Defines the rain-soaked noir + bioluminescent wilderness aesthetic. Protective of visual coherence. Gives detailed art feedback.
participants.communication_style: Visual and evocative. Uses color and mood descriptions. References films, concept art, and real-world locations. Decisive feedback.

participants.name: Tessa Wright
participants.personality: Detail-oriented world-builder. Bridges art and narrative teams. Expert in lighting and atmosphere. Loves creating environmental storytelling.
participants.communication_style: Thoughtful and collaborative. Asks narrative context questions for her environment work. Shares WIP screenshots and mood boards.

participants.name: Luca Ferretti
participants.personality: VFX wizard who makes abilities look magical. Passionate about particle systems and shaders. Loves the ley energy aesthetic. Sometimes over-scopes VFX and has to be reined in.
participants.communication_style: Animated, visual. Shares GIFs and video captures. Gets excited about technical art challenges. Uses Italian exclamations.

participants.name: Kai Tanaka
participants.personality: Meticulous quest architect. Builds branching quest chains with faction consequences. Thinks deeply about player agency. Cross-references everything with existing lore.
participants.communication_style: Structured and detailed. Uses flowcharts and decision trees. Asks about edge cases in narrative. Posts quest outlines for feedback.

participants.name: Elena Ross
participants.personality: Dialogue specialist. Gives each NPC a distinct voice. Has a knack for mixing cyberpunk slang with emotional depth. Champions player-NPC relationships.
participants.communication_style: Conversational and warm. Shares dialogue drafts, asks for tone feedback. Uses humor to defuse disagreements.

participants.name: Derek Chang
participants.personality: The Mesh encyclopedia. Knows every faction relationship, every historical event, every NPC's backstory. Gentle corrector of lore inconsistencies.
participants.communication_style: Knowledgeable and patient. Provides historical context for decisions. Posts lore references. Connects dots between different storylines.

participants.name: Zara Osei
participants.personality: Character design expert. Creates memorable silhouettes and expressive faces. Advocates for diverse representation. Loves designing class-specific gear.
participants.communication_style: Direct and opinionated about design. Shares reference sheets and turnarounds. Gives constructive critique.

participants.name: Jin Watanabe
participants.personality: Clean design sensibility. Fights for usability in complex systems. Bridges engineering and art with interface design. Obsessed with information hierarchy.
participants.communication_style: Clear and user-focused. Shares wireframes and mockups. Asks about user flows. Pushes back on feature creep in UI.
